diagrams.title: Honeypot Network Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the typical deployment topology of honeypots within a network security infrastructure, illustrating how honeypots are isolated from production systems while being accessible to potential attackers.
diagrams.purpose: Explains where honeypots are positioned in network architecture and how they are segmented to detect attackers while protecting real assets

diagrams.title: Honeypot Detection Process Flow
diagrams.description: Illustrates the step-by-step process of how honeypots attract, detect, and record attacker activity, from initial reconnaissance through to alert generation and forensic data collection.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the lifecycle of intrusion detection in honeypots and how they capture attacker techniques without risking production systems

diagrams.title: Honeypot Interaction Levels
diagrams.description: Shows the spectrum of honeypot types from low interaction (emulated services) to high interaction (real operating systems), highlighting the trade-offs between security risk and data收集.
diagrams.purpose: Compares different honeypot types and helps security professionals choose appropriate honeypot configurations based on their detection and research needs
